headlines.summary: Higher oil prices drove the first-quarter adjusted earnings at ExxonMobil (NYSE: XOM) above the analyst estimates as the jump in prices more than offset lower oil and gas production in the Middle East and Kazakhstan. headlines.title: Pakistan Imports First LNG Cargo in Weeks in Relief for Energy Crisis
headlines.summary: Pakistan has welcomed the first LNG cargo in almost two months as an LNG carrier docked at the GasPort terminal in what would be the first relief to the energy crisis in the country. headlines.title: Iran Tensions Send WTI Crude Surging Above $105
headlines.summary: June WTI crude oil futures surged this week, with the contract trading at $105.88 Thursday night, up $11.48 or 12.16%. The weekly high is $110.93, while the low is $94.59. headlines.title: U.S. Oil and Gas Firms Sign Deals to Operate in Venezuela
headlines.summary: Venezuela has signed agreements with two U.S. energy firms to operate in the country as a growing number of international oil and gas companies are vying to return to the world's biggest crude resource holder. headlines.title: TotalEnergies Extends Fuel Price Cap in France as Middle East Crisis Drags On
headlines.summary: TotalEnergies will keep its fuel prices in France capped for as long as the Middle East crisis lasts, the French supermajor said, extending the policy first introduced two weeks into the conflict. headlines.title: Woodside Struggles to Secure Buyers for U.S. LNG as Pricing Backfires
headlines.summary: Australia’s Woodside Energy is having trouble finding buyers for the liquefied natural gas it produces at its Louisiana LNG plant, Reuters has reported, citing unnamed sources. headlines.title: Indian Oil Raises LPG and Jet Fuel Prices
headlines.summary: Indian Oil Co. has hiked the price of liquefied petroleum gas for local industrial users and the price of jet fuel, but only for foreign airlines, Reuters has reported, citing a company statement.
